articles.title: Sapiom CEO: Software is becoming the ‘customer' of the Internet
articles.summary: Sapiom CEO argues that AI agents are becoming the primary "customers" of the internet, raising identity and authentication challenges as traditional IAM models built for humans struggle to accommodate non-human software identities.

articles.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
articles.summary: AppViewX CEO discusses how AI agent identities are converging with machine identity management, highlighting growing governance and visibility challenges that IAM professionals must address as non-human identities proliferate.

articles.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
articles.summary: This article argues that AI agents are emerging as highly privileged identities that expose critical gaps in traditional PAM controls, advocating for just-in-time access and ephemeral credentials as the new standard for managing non-human identities.

articles.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
articles.summary: Google's Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 binds session cookies to hardware keys on Windows, directly countering infostealer-based session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA and SSO protections—a significant development for IAM professionals managing browser-based authentication security.

articles.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
articles.summary: Google Threat Intelligence Group warns of UNC6783, a threat actor using fake Okta login pages and social engineering impersonation tactics to breach corporate networks and exfiltrate sensitive data, directly threatening organizations relying on Okta for identity management.

articles.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
articles.summary: Google Chrome is shipping device-bound session credentials (DBSC) to cryptographically tie authentication cookies to specific devices, directly countering infostealer-based session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA and SSO protections.

articles.title: Google Chrome 146 Adds Device Bound Session Credentials to Stop Session Cookie Theft on Windows
articles.summary: Google Chrome 146 introduces Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) for Windows, cryptographically binding session cookies to device hardware—directly addressing session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA and represent a critical threat vector IAM professionals must account for in their authentication strategies.

articles.title: Google Chrome adds infostealer protection against session cookie theft
articles.summary: Google Chrome 146 introduces Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to cryptographically bind session cookies to a specific device, directly addressing session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA and authentication controls — a critical threat vector for IAM practitioners.

articles.title: 'Several dozen' high-value corporations hit by new extortion crew in helpdesk phishing spree
articles.summary: A new extortion group is compromising enterprise accounts by social-engineering IT helpdesks—a direct attack vector against identity verification processes that IAM teams need to defend against, consistent with tactics used in high-profile MFA bypass incidents.

articles.title: Protecting Cookies with Device Bound Session Credentials
articles.summary: Google's Chrome team is advancing Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC), a security mechanism that cryptographically binds session cookies to specific devices, directly addressing session hijacking attacks that can bypass MFA and SSO protections.

articles.title: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
articles.summary: Microsoft DART researchers detail Storm-2755, a financially motivated threat actor compromising Canadian employee accounts to redirect payroll, highlighting critical risks around account takeover, identity-based attacks, and the need for strong MFA and access controls in enterprise environments.

articles.title: When attackers already have the keys, MFA is just another door to open
articles.summary: Analysis of the Figure breach (967,200 email records exposed) argues that stolen credentials render traditional MFA ineffective and advocates for wearable biometric authentication that verifies users rather than sessions to block phishing relays and MFA bypass attacks—directly relevant to IAM architects reconsidering authentication strategies.

articles.title: Keycloak 26.6 bringt Zero-Downtime-Updates und Workflows
articles.summary: Keycloak 26.6 releases with five features graduating to production status, including Federated Client Authentication and Zero-Downtime Updates, which are significant operational improvements for organizations running this widely-used open-source IAM platform.

articles.title: AI agent intent is a starting point, not a security strategy
articles.summary: Token Security research reveals 65% of agentic AI chatbots hold live access credentials despite never being used, highlighting critical non-human identity and credential sprawl risks that IAM professionals must address as agentic AI adoption grows.

articles.title: Microsoft Confirms New And Widespread 2FA Code Attacks Ongoing
articles.summary: Microsoft has confirmed an active, widespread MFA device code attack campaign ("EvilTokens") compromising hundreds of organizations daily, representing a critical threat to authentication security that IAM professionals must address immediately.

articles.title: Auth0 Metric Streams（Beta）でRPSをNew Relicで監視してみた
articles.summary: A technical tutorial (in Japanese) demonstrates how to monitor Auth0 authentication requests-per-second (RPS) metrics using the beta Metric Streams feature integrated with New Relicobservability platform, relevant to teams managing Auth0 deployments.

articles.title: What managing partners should ask AI vendors before signing any contract
articles.summary: TMF Group's CSRO argues that over-privileged access and weak workflow controls represent a greater threat than ransomware, offering IAM-relevant guidance on evaluating AI vendor contracts for access control risks.

articles.title: Hundreds of orgs compromised daily in Microsoft device code phishing attacks
articles.summary: A sophisticated phishing campaign is compromising hundreds of organizations daily by exploiting Microsoft's device code authentication flow to steal OAuth tokens, effectively bypassing MFA and granting persistent access to corporate email—a critical threat to IAM defenders relying on Microsoft Entra/Azure AD authentication.

articles.title: Agentic AI's role in amplifying and creating insider risks
articles.summary: Agentic AI is emerging as a distinct insider risk vector requiring IAM professionals to extend identity governance, access controls, and monitoring frameworks to cover non-human AI agent identities alongside human users.

articles.title: Show HN: QRAuth – Open-source QR verification with passkeys and device trust
articles.summary: An open-source identity verification platform called QRAuth has launched, combining QR codes, WebAuthn passkeys, and device trust into a single SDK for passwordless authentication and physical access control — potentially useful for IAM practitioners evaluating passkey implementations, but as an early-stage open-source project with limited adoption signals.
